title: PARSEFILE
text: PARSEFILE Parses an XML document in the specified file. Explain this code PARSEFILE(FileName,Validation) Copy Parameters Table 1. PARSEFILE parameters Parameter Data Type Direction Description FileName String IN Name of the file containing the XML document to be parsed; maximum size is 512 bytes. The file name is resolved according to the assignment file [FILES] settings, so the file can be local or remote. The encoding of the XML document can be UTF-8 or another form of encoding (in contrast with PARSE ). Validation Boolean IN Determines whether the XML parser will validate the XML it reads. Return Values If a fatal error occurs during parsing, -151 is returned, unless the FATALERROR operation returns a different negative value. In this case that value is returned instead. Table 2. Number Error Description 0 Success –57 <UACTERR_NO_INSTANCE> No handler instance set. If you invoke the PARSE or PARSEFILE operation without having set a handler instance, they will return this error and will not parse anything. –151 <UACTERR_EXCEPTION_THROWN> A fatal error has occured but there is no FATALERROR operation implemented. A FATALERROR operation is available but it returns nothing or a value >= 0. A fatal error is deemed unrecoverable, by the SAX parser. Description The PARSEFILE operation invokes the SAX parser built into Uniface and the SAX operations of the handler instance. If any handler operation returns a negative value, parsing stops and PARSE returns with that negative value. Explain this code activate $50.setHandler($instancename) activate $50.parsefile("myxml.xml", "T") Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
